// fixture_developer_facing.go.txt: synthetic source with strings that
// LOOK like prose but live in exempt contexts (errors, logging, test
// helpers, comments). Walker must report ZERO findings here.
package fixture

import (
	"errors"
	"fmt"
	"log"
	"log/slog"
)

var ErrSomething = errors.New("Database connection refused by the host")

func wrapErr(name string) error {
	return fmt.Errorf("failed to open file %s for reading: not a directory", name)
}

func logSomething() {
	log.Printf("Worker pool initialised with %d shards", 4)
	slog.Info("Replication lag exceeded threshold")
}

// CONST-046: the following struct tag is metadata, not content.
type Payload struct {
	Foo string `json:"foo_bar_baz_field"`
}
